string.description: Primitive type for immutable sequences of UTF-16 characters

number.description: 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point, the only numeric type

boolean.description: Logical data type with exactly two values: true and false

bigint.description: Arbitrary-precision integer type created with BigInt() or the n suffix

symbol.description: Unique and immutable identifier, often used as object property keys

undefined.description: Default value for uninitialized variables, missing parameters, and absent properties

null.description: Intentional absence of any object value, typeof returns 'object'

Object.description: Base prototype for all objects; provides keys, values, entries, assign, freeze methods

Array.description: Ordered collection of values with integer indices and rich iteration methods

Map.description: Key-value collection that maintains insertion order and allows any key type

Set.description: Collection of unique values of any type with O(1) membership testing

Promise.description: Represents the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation

Date.description: Represents a single moment in time with millisecond precision since Unix epoch

RegExp.description: Regular expression object for pattern matching with test, exec, and match methods

Error.description: Base class for runtime errors with message, name, and stack properties

Function.description: Every function is an instance of Function, supports call, apply, and bind

WeakMap.description: Collection of key-value pairs where keys are weakly referenced objects

TypedArray.description: Array-like view of a binary ArrayBuffer with typed element access
